title: Lesson 6 - Platform-Specific Enhancements
description: Respect the open standard first, then layer optional features for Claude Code, GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and other tools — without breaking portability.
learningObjectives: Use Claude Code extensions: $ARGUMENTS, paths-based auto-activation, context forking, and invocation controls.
Understand how GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and Gemini CLI detect and load skills.
Design skills with a portability-first mindset so platform features degrade gracefully.
Document compatibility directly in SKILL.md frontmatter.

videos.title: Claude Code: The Most Feature-Rich Platform
videos.description: Explore Claude Code's powerful extensions and show how to add them incrementally to the deploy-checklist skill.
scriptOutline: Claude Code supports all standard features PLUS the richest set of extensions.
User invocation: /deploy-checklist from the command line — like a slash command.
$ARGUMENTS: accept user input like /deploy-checklist staging. Show positional args ($1, $ARGUMENTS[0]).
paths for auto-activation: paths: ['scripts/deploy/**'] makes the skill auto-load when editing deploy files.
context: fork — run the skill in an isolated subprocess for heavy analysis without polluting the conversation.
Dynamic context injection: !`git diff --staged` embeds live system state into the skill.
Running example: add paths and $ARGUMENTS to deploy-checklist so it auto-activates and accepts a target environment.
Invocation controls: user-invocable (show/hide from / menu), disable-model-invocation (prevent auto-triggering).
talkingPoints: Claude Code pioneered most of these extensions — it has the richest feature set in the ecosystem.
$ARGUMENTS is the most useful: /deploy-checklist production lets users pass context without extra typing.
paths-based auto-activation is magic: the skill loads itself when you touch relevant files.
context: fork protects heavy skills from polluting your main conversation.

videos.title: GitHub Copilot, Cursor, and the Broader Ecosystem
videos.description: How other platforms handle skills, where they store them, and which extensions they support.
scriptOutline: GitHub Copilot: checks .github/skills/, .claude/skills/, AND .agents/skills/ — your Claude Code skills often just work.
Copilot surfaces: cloud agent, CLI, VS Code agent mode — same skill works across all three.
Cursor: supports .cursor/skills/ and .claude/skills/ — reads most standard fields.
Gemini CLI + JetBrains: follow the standard via .agents/skills/.
The convergence: 30+ tools now read the same format. Your skills are more portable than ever.
When in doubt: drop the skill into .agents/skills/ for maximum compatibility.
talkingPoints: GitHub Copilot checking .claude/skills/ means your Claude Code skills work in Copilot without changes.
The ecosystem convergence is real — tools are competing on features, not on format.
If you're not sure which directory to use, .agents/skills/ is the universal fallback.

videos.title: The Portability-First Playbook
videos.description: A disciplined approach: build on the standard first, add platform features as opt-in enhancements.
scriptOutline: Strategy: standard features first (Lessons 1-5), platform features on top (this lesson).
Platform features are additive: tools that don't support them simply ignore them — no errors.
Fallback behavior: design your skill to work without $ARGUMENTS or paths (provide defaults).
The compatibility field: document which platforms you've tested.
Running example: deploy-checklist now works everywhere AND has Claude Code auto-activation. Best of both worlds.
Troubleshooting checklist: what to check when a skill works on one platform but not another.
talkingPoints: A great skill works everywhere. Platform features make it shine on specific tools.
Platform extensions are ignored (not errors) on tools that don't support them — safe to add.
Test on two platforms minimum. You'll catch subtle path and script issues.
Use the compatibility field honestly: list only platforms you've actually tested.

textSections.title: Claude Code Extensions Reference
textSections.content:
**Frontmatter extensions (Claude Code only):**

```yaml
---
name: deploy-checklist
description: Run pre-deploy validations before shipping backend services
paths:                            # Auto-activate for matching files
  - "scripts/deploy/**"
  - "infrastructure/**"
argument-hint: <environment>      # Help text shown in / menu
user-invocable: true              # Show in / menu (default: true)
disable-model-invocation: false   # Allow AI to auto-trigger (default: false)
context: fork                     # Run in isolated subprocess
model: sonnet                     # Override model for this skill
---
```

**String substitutions:**

| Variable | Meaning | Example |
|----------|---------|--------|
| `$ARGUMENTS` | All user input after `/skill-name` | `/deploy-checklist staging` → `staging` |
| `$ARGUMENTS[0]` | First positional argument | Same → `staging` |
| `$1` | Shorthand for first argument | Same → `staging` |
| `${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}` | Path to the skill's directory | For referencing bundled files |

**Dynamic context injection:**
```markdown
Current staged changes:
!`git diff --staged`

Current branch:
!`git branch --show-current`
```
The !\`command\` syntax runs the command and injects its output into the prompt when the skill loads.

textSections.title: Platform Compatibility Matrix
textSections.content:
| Feature | Open Standard | Claude Code | Copilot | Cursor | Gemini CLI |
|---------|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|:---:|
| SKILL.md + frontmatter | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| scripts/ references/ assets/ | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes | Yes |
| /skill-name invocation | — | Yes | — | — | — |
| $ARGUMENTS | — | Yes | — | — | — |
| paths auto-activation | — | Yes | — | — | — |
| context: fork | — | Yes | — | — | — |
| Dynamic context !\`\` | — | Yes | — | — | — |
| Multiple skill directories | — | — | Yes | Yes | — |

**Key insight:** The standard features (top two rows) work everywhere. Platform features (bottom rows) are bonus.

textSections.title: Running Example: deploy-checklist with Platform Enhancements
textSections.content:
Here's the deploy-checklist frontmatter after adding Claude Code features:

```markdown
---
name: deploy-checklist
description: Run pre-deploy validations before shipping backend services to production
paths:
  - "scripts/deploy/**"
  - "infrastructure/**"
  - "**/Dockerfile"
argument-hint: <environment>
compatibility: Tested on Claude Code 1.x, GitHub Copilot (VS Code)
---

# Deploy Checklist

## Trigger
Activate when deploying backend services.
Target environment: $ARGUMENTS (default to 'staging' if not specified).

## Workflow
1. Run `scripts/pre-deploy-check.sh`
2. Read `assets/required-env-vars.json` — verify vars for the target environment
...
```

**On Claude Code:** `/deploy-checklist production` auto-activates near deploy files, accepts environment as argument.
**On Copilot/Cursor:** Works identically — just activated manually or by AI matching the description. $ARGUMENTS ignored gracefully.

textSections.title: Troubleshooting Cross-Platform Issues
textSections.content:
When a skill works on one platform but not another:

1. **Check the directory path** — is the skill in the right location for that tool?
2. **Validate frontmatter** — YAML typos silently break activation.
3. **Test without platform features** — strip Claude Code extensions and test the baseline.
4. **Check script compatibility** — bash scripts may behave differently on macOS vs. Linux vs. Windows.
5. **Review logs** — many tools show which skills they discovered at startup.

Add findings to your compatibility notes. Future you (and your team) will thank you.

commonMistakes.explanation: Start with the standard. If your skill relies on $ARGUMENTS to function, it won't work in Copilot or Cursor. Standard first, enhancements second.
$ARGUMENTS, context: fork, and paths are Claude Code-only. They're silently ignored (not errors) on other platforms, but your skill must work without them.

commonMistakes.mistake: Not providing defaults for platform features
commonMistakes.explanation: If $ARGUMENTS is empty (non-Claude Code tools), your skill should still work. Add a default: 'Target environment: $ARGUMENTS (default to staging if not specified).'
!`command` runs on every skill load. Keep commands fast and outputs small. !`git log --oneline -1000` will bloat the context.
If you say 'works on Claude Code and Copilot', test on both. Use the compatibility field to document what you've actually verified.

reflectionQuestions: Which platform feature would benefit your skill the most? Why?
Does your skill still work if ALL platform-specific features are removed?
If a teammate uses a different AI tool than you, can they still use your skill?
How would you test your skill on a platform you don't normally use?

exercises.title: Add One Platform Enhancement
exercises.description: Choose a platform feature (e.g., $ARGUMENTS, paths, context: fork) and add it to your skill. Test it end-to-end.
exercises.output: Updated SKILL.md frontmatter plus test evidence showing the feature working.
successCriteria: Platform feature added to frontmatter or instruction body.
Skill still works WITHOUT the platform feature (graceful degradation tested).
Test log shows the feature working on your primary tool.
Usage documented inside SKILL.md (e.g., how to pass arguments).

exercises.title: Cross-Platform Smoke Test
exercises.description: Test your skill on a second platform or mode (e.g., Copilot if you built in Claude Code, or vice versa). Log any differences.
exercises.output: Compatibility notes documenting what worked and what differed.
successCriteria: Tested on at least one additional platform.
Core workflow verified working without platform extensions.
Updated the compatibility field in frontmatter.

exercises.title: Write Compatibility Documentation
exercises.description: Add the compatibility field to frontmatter and document platform-specific usage in SKILL.md.
exercises.output: Updated SKILL.md with compatibility info and platform-specific usage notes.
successCriteria: Compatibility field lists platforms and versions tested.
Any platform-specific features documented with usage examples.
Fallback behavior described for tools that don't support extensions.

resources.title: Claude Code Skills — Full Feature Reference

instructorNotes: This lesson is more reference-oriented. Don't try to cover every feature — focus on the 3-4 most useful: $ARGUMENTS, paths, and context: fork.
For Claude Code users: /skill-name invocation is the 'wow' moment. Demo it live with the deploy-checklist.
For mixed-tool audiences: the portability-first strategy validates everyone's tool choice. Nobody feels like a second-class citizen.
The compatibility matrix is a great reference to keep visible throughout exercises.
Dynamic context injection (!`command`) is advanced. Only cover it in depth if the audience is comfortable with the command line.
Pair learners by tool preference for the cross-platform exercise — they can test each other's skills.
Remind learners: platform features are bonus, not requirements. A great standard skill beats a mediocre platform-specific one.
